name: Hot Fajita Wings with Guacamole
recipeIngredient: 2 1/2 pounds Chicken wings, shoulder and midsection only -- cut apart
1 carton (6 oz) frozen avocado dip, thawed
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large plastic bag, mix honey, lime juice, chili powder, and soy sauce.
2. Add the chicken wings; seal the bag and mix to coat evenly.
3. Chill the wings, turning occasionally, for at least 1 hour or up to a day.
4. Preheat the broiler on two baking pans, each about 10 by 15 inches.
5. Place the wings in a single layer on the prepared pans.
6. Bake the wings at 450 degrees Fahrenheit for 15 minutes.
7. Turn the wing pieces over and continue baking until they are browned, about 25 to 30 minutes.
8. If making ahead of time, let the wings cool, wrap them tightly, and chill up to 1 day; serve at room temperature or reheat in a single layer on the pans at 350 degrees Fahrenheit for about 10 minutes.
9. Place the wings on a platter and set the avocado dip beside them.
Makes 8 to 10 appetizer servings. Per serving: 170 calories (53% from fat), 10 g fat (2.4 g saturated), 363 mg sodium, 29 mg cholesterol.
